_doc: Daily market-state snapshot. Written by the close-of-market run, read by the next morning run (and the next close run for day-over-day deltas). Reasoning fuel — the reader never sees this file, only the conclusions drawn from it. Fields left null were not collected; collect them next run rather than guessing.
session_note: THE BREAKOUT HELD THE LINE BUT THE INSIDE BROKE. SPX closed 7,483.23 (-0.2% / -16.1 vs 7,499.36) — still ABOVE the reclaimed ~7,450 zero-gamma flip (breakout technically intact), pinned just under the 7,500 call wall again. Dow 52,305.24 (-14 pts), Nasdaq ~26,040 (-0.66%), VIX 16.59 (+0.14). But the index MASKED a violent supply-down / demand-up rotation: the AI-hardware/memory/neocloud complex was repriced while mega-cap PLATFORMS became the defensive bid. Model book (Yahoo chart API, Jul 1 vs Jun 30): MU -10.57% ($1,032.28, WORST), ALAB -10.80% ($430.86), MRVL -8.67% ($272.05), VRT -6.99% ($311.42), TSM -6.98% ($444.23), CRWV -13.92% ($85.68), AVGO -2.23% ($369.34), DELL -1.44% ($425.25), NVDA -1.25% ($197.58, back UNDER $200) — vs GOOGL +1.07% ($361.21) and MAGS +2.39% ($65.84, the Mag-7 ETF carried by the platforms). Tracked: MSFT +3.02% ($384.28), AAPL +1.73% ($294.38, RECLAIMED the $293.08 memory-margin line), META +8.81% ($612.91), NBIS -17.01% ($229.18, worst neocloud), AMD -6.89% ($540.88), SOXX -6.41% ($599.70), QCOM -1.55% ($181.92), IGV $93.34 (+3.0%), SPY 745.76, QQQ 725.17 (-1.52%). Estimated model-portfolio P&L ~-3.0% on a flat index. THE CATALYST: Bloomberg reported Meta is building a cloud business ('Meta Compute') to sell EXCESS AI compute (META +8.8%) — which adds GPU supply, pressures rental pricing, and threatens the neocloud scarcity premium (CRWV/NBIS both count Meta as a backstop customer). Stacked with a hard tell (B200 cloud rental rates -31% MoM) and rate pressure (~50bp of Fed hikes now priced by December under Warsh), the AI-capex-payback/oversupply DOUBT that was out-shouted by the Iran-relief bid last week finally DROVE tape. Warsh (Sintra) was hawkish-leaning but mixed ('prices too high' / would 'disappoint' anyone expecting >2% tolerance / no forward guidance — but 'inflation risks have come down'); a sideshow to the oversupply story (10Y only +0.10, mega-caps UP). ADP +98K (below +110K est, cooling) into Thursday's BLS jobs (pulled early; markets closed Fri Jul 3).

spx.note: Close 7,483.23 (-0.2% / -16.1 vs 7,499.36). OHLC approximate (open/high/low estimated from a small-range -0.2% session; the reliable numbers are the close and the -0.2% day-change from search + SPY x10 cross-check: SPY 745.76 x10 = 7,457.6, ~25 pts under SPX, within documented drift). CRITICAL: SPX HELD above the reclaimed ~7,450 flip and stalled just under the 7,500 call wall — the breakout is intact at the index level (positive-gamma pin held: -0.2% range, VIX 16.59), but it did NOT extend toward 7,510, and beneath the flat tape was a violent supply/demand rotation. Resolves flip-now-support-holds RIGHT for July 1 (open, one session left).

nasdaq_composite.note: ~-0.66% (est ~26,040 from 26,213.72). Tech gave back — QQQ -1.52% to $725.17. The mega-cap platforms (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/META) UP masked a semis crash (SOXX -6.41%) at the composite level; the Nasdaq-100 (QQQ) fell more (-1.52%) because chips are a bigger QQQ weight.

dow.note: -14 pts to 52,305.24 — essentially flat; comms/financials did the lifting (value-ish), the AI-hardware weakness barely touches the price-weighted Dow.

regime.rationale: DOWNGRADED from bull (June 30) back to bull-under-stress. The index-level breakout HELD — SPX closed 7,483.23 above the reclaimed ~7,450 flip, VIX low (16.59), positive-gamma pin intact (a -0.2% range that stalled under the 7,500 call wall). That keeps it out of bear-rally/bear. BUT the leadership fractured violently: the AI-supply complex that IS the thesis (memory/chips/neoclouds) was repriced on a REAL supply-side catalyst (Meta Compute selling excess compute + B200 rental rates -31% MoM), not sentiment, and the model book took ~-3% on a flat index. This is stress under the surface — the AI-capex-payback doubt drove tape for the first time instead of being out-shouted. Not a 'bull' (leadership broke, our book fell hard); not 'bear' (index held the flip, platforms bid, VIX calm). Bull-under-stress captures it: the machine held the line while the engine got repriced.
regime.what_changes_it: UP to bull on a close above 7,510 with the AI-supply complex STABILIZING (SOXX reclaiming ground, neoclouds basing) — i.e. today's rotation proves a one-day event, not a de-rate. DOWN to bear-rally/bear on a CLOSE below ~7,450 (the reclaimed flip fails → breakout was a quarter-end head-fake) or below 7,300 (the twice-defended shelf), OR a SECOND oversupply catalyst (another hyperscaler announces compute-reselling / a capex GUIDE cut at the late-July Q2 prints — the T1 master switch). Watch: (1) do the AI-hardware names keep de-rating (Meta-Compute a durable supply shift) or snap back (a positioning flush like late June); (2) does SPX hold 7,450 through Thursday's BLS jobs; (3) does the platform-over-supply rotation persist (a regime tell) or mean-revert.

key_levels.notes: 7,450 = the reclaimed zero-gamma flip, now FIRST support (held today on a closing basis, SPX 7,483). 7,394 = the migrated-down gamma flip / character-change line below which gamma flips negative again (dealers amplify). 7,300 = twice-defended put-side shelf; 7,265 = June 10/11 hold. Resistance: 7,500 = the call wall SPX pinned just under twice now (Jun 30 close 7,499, Jul 1 close 7,483); 7,510 = the mid-June broken floor; 7,600 = June 1 record (7,599.96). The pin under 7,500 two sessions running is the positive-gamma tell — dealers sell into the wall.

gamma.source: CARRIED / STRUCTURAL (market_levels.py hung again — no computed feed; no fresh July-1 snippet found, only stale June-29 data). Reasoned reframe: SPX closed 7,483.23, ABOVE the reclaimed 7,450 flip and pinned just under the 7,500 call wall for the 2nd straight session, in a -0.2% / low-VIX (16.59) range — the signature of an INTACT positive-gamma pin (dealers dampening, selling into 7,500). So positive gamma is very likely still stacked (June-30 snippet had net GEX ~+$23.95B), but no FRESH net-GEX number was collectable today — left null rather than guessed (a null is information). Operative levels: 7,450 first support (held), 7,394 the flip below which gamma re-flips negative, 7,500 the wall overhead. near_put_wall set to 7,450 (the reclaimed flip / first ledge dip-buyers defend), near_call_wall 7,500 (the pinned wall).
gamma.note: market_levels.py UNAVAILABLE (0-byte hang, killed — the documented intermittent failure mode, back-to-back with June 30). Closes/day-changes for all 11 holdings + SPY/QQQ/SOXX/AAPL/MSFT/QCOM/META/NBIS/AMD via the Yahoo chart API (5d range, validated last-bar dates = 2026-07-01). factor_tape.py (Yahoo) ran clean for cross-asset.
gamma.computed_rejected: market_levels.py hung at 0-byte output twice (original + a 200s-timeout retry) and was killed both times — no computed flip/walls/net-GEX/audit block produced. Snippet search returned only stale June-29 levels (flip 7,408 / call wall 7,400 / net GEX -2.3B, all pre-breakout and inconsistent with today's 7,483 close). Nothing fresh to record; structural reframe governs.
gamma.audit_log: 2026-07-01: market_levels.py --audit HUNG (0-byte, killed) on both the initial run and a fresh 200s-timeout retry — the intermittent-hang failure mode, now two sessions running (June 30 also hung). Side-by-side trust period cannot progress. Snippet crosscheck yielded only stale June-29 data (unusable). Cross-asset (factor_tape.py, Yahoo, clean): 10Y 4.47% (+0.10 abs / +2.36%, 5d -0.03 falling), WTI 68.09 (-2.03%, 5d -2.25 falling), DXY 101.39 (+0.20, 5d -0.22), VIX 16.59 (+0.14 / +0.85%, 5d -2.04 falling), gold 4044.6 (+0.54%, 5d +54.3 RISING — a mild hedge bid returned).

rates_commodities.note: 10Y 4.47% (+0.10 abs / +2.36% today, 5d -0.03) — yields ROSE on the day (the biggest single-factor move), consistent with Warsh's 'prices too high' + ~50bp-of-hikes-by-December repricing. But the equity damage was NOT a duration story (mega-cap growth ROSE); rates were secondary to the AI-oversupply rotation. WTI $68.09 (-2.03%, 5d -2.25 falling) — crude soft, Iran de-escalation holding, no war premium. Gold $4,044.60 (+0.54%, 5d +54.3 RISING) — a mild hedge/debasement bid RETURNED after two sessions of unwind, the first cross-asset caution tell since the breakout. DXY 101.39. From factor_tape.py (Yahoo).

volatility_flow.vix_note: VIX 16.59 (+0.14 / +0.85%, 5d -2.04 falling) — essentially flat, still sub-17. The KEY read: a -10% memory day / -6% semis day with VIX barely moving confirms this was a ROTATION, not a broad de-risking — index-level vol stayed low because the platform strength offset the chip carnage. A genuine market-wide selloff of that AI-hardware magnitude would have spiked VIX; it didn't. Intraday not separately collected.
volatility_flow.put_call_note: Not collected (lags 1-2 days free). Structural read: VIX sub-17 with SPX pinned under the 7,500 call wall = positive-gamma pin intact at the index level despite the internal rotation.
volatility_flow.note: No corroborated single large print. Cleanest flow read is structural: SPX pinned the 7,500 call wall for the 2nd straight session (Jun 30 close 7,499, Jul 1 close 7,483) — the positive-gamma 'dealers sell into the wall' pin held even as the sub-surface rotation raged. The day's real flow story was the sector rotation (AI-supply out, platforms in), not index-level positioning.

technicals.breadth_note: NARROW/SPLIT — a textbook rotation, not broad participation. DOWN hard (AI-supply): MU -10.57% $1,032.28 (WORST book name), ALAB -10.80% $430.86, MRVL -8.67% $272.05, VRT -6.99% $311.42, TSM -6.98% $444.23, CRWV -13.92% $85.68, AVGO -2.23% $369.34, NVDA -1.25% $197.58, DELL -1.44% $425.25. UP (platforms + Mag-7 ETF): GOOGL +1.07% $361.21, MAGS +2.39% $65.84. Tracked: MSFT +3.02% $384.28, AAPL +1.73% $294.38 (RECLAIMED $293.08), META +8.81% $612.91 (the catalyst), NBIS -17.01% $229.18 (worst neocloud), AMD -6.89% $540.88, SOXX -6.41% $599.70, QCOM -1.55% $181.92, IGV $93.34 (+3.0%, above the $88 software line). Estimated model-book P&L ~-3.0% on the day. All the damage concentrated in the AI picks-and-shovels; the index held because the platforms (which we largely DON'T own outright, only via MAGS + GOOGL) rose.
technicals.note: DMAs drift-estimated (market_levels.py hung, no direct read). SPY x10 (7,457.6) ran ~25 pts under SPX (7,483) — use SPX as the index read.

factor_attribution.dominant_driver: A clean and important driver: AI-capex-payback / OVERSUPPLY doubt, catalyzed by the Meta-Compute report (Meta building a cloud biz to sell EXCESS AI compute), stacked on B200 rental rates -31% MoM and ~50bp-of-hikes-by-December rate pressure — driving a violent rotation OUT of the AI-supply complex (chips/memory/neoclouds) INTO the mega-cap platforms (the defensive AI trade). NOT a rates/duration story (10Y rose but mega-cap growth ROSE); NOT a war story (oil soft, Iran quiet).
factor_attribution.detail: The tell that isolates the driver: on a day yields ROSE (+0.10) and Warsh said 'prices too high,' the LONGEST-duration mega-cap growth (MSFT +3%, AAPL +1.7%, GOOGL +1.1%, META +8.8%) OUTPERFORMED — the exact opposite of a duration selloff. So the hit was NOT rates; it was the AI-supply complex repricing on a supply-side catalyst. Meta reselling excess compute threatens the neocloud scarcity premium (CRWV -13.9%, NBIS -17%) and validates the falling-lease-rate signal that the whole 'is the capex worth it' doubt rests on. The platforms are the DEFENSIVE AI trade on capex-doubt days because (a) they'd benefit from cheaper compute, (b) they're the demand side, (c) Meta specifically got REWARDED for monetizing infra. This is the 3rd time the supply-over-demand thesis has run inverse on a capex-DOUBT day (June 23, June 26 partial, July 1) — the regime-dependence pattern (chips lead on risk-on/demand-confident days, platforms lead on capex-doubt days) is now well established. Cross-asset (factor_tape.py, Yahoo): 10Y 4.47 (+2.36%), WTI 68.09 (-2.03%), DXY 101.39, VIX 16.59 (+0.85%), gold 4044.6 (+0.54% RISING = mild hedge bid back).

calendar.event: June jobs report (BLS, 8:30 ET; pulled early — markets CLOSED Fri Jul 3 for July 4 observed) + software-rotation cluster horizon
calendar.why: The week's macro binary. ADP printed +98K (cooling); consensus for BLS ~110K, 4.3% unemployment. Hot -> rate fear stacks on the AI-oversupply doubt (double hit to the supply complex); cool -> broad relief. GOVERNS: 2026-06-25-jobs-duration-branch (SOXX vs mega-cap-growth on a HOT print), 2026-06-30-flip-now-support-holds (no SPX close below 7,450), 2026-06-26-floor-holds-into-jobs (no close below 7,300), and the software cluster (software-quarter-end-fade IGV<$84.76 both Jul 1+2 — Jul 1 was $93.34, tracking hard toward falsified; software-squeeze CRM+DUOL vs VEEV+NOW cumulative).
Two sessions after the jobs print — SOXX vs mega-cap-growth basket on a hot print.
AAPL closed $294.38, ABOVE the $293.08 line — the memory-BUYER rallied on a memory-shortage-doubt day; resolved early. No longer a live horizon.
Two-week window to test whether today's AI-supply repricing is a durable de-rate (SOXX doesn't reclaim $640.76; CRWV doesn't reclaim $99.54) or another dip that snaps back like the late-June flushes.

Next hard foundry/yield checkpoint — external-foundry revenue + 18A/18A-P yield-at-volume; also the first mega-cap-adjacent print of the late-July Q2 season that carries the T1 capex-guide master switch.

THE T1 master switch — the first hyperscaler capex GUIDE cut (with cloud-rev deceleration + shrinking RPO) is the AI-capex-cycle falsifier. Today's Meta-Compute oversupply signal makes these prints the key read on whether the buildout is topping.

Supply-side basket vs demand-side platforms from June 1. July 1 was a HARD inverse counter-print (supply -6/-14%, platforms up) — the 3rd, all on capex-doubt days. Thesis intact (Sept-1 horizon) but the regime-dependence is a confirmed pattern; watch the cluster.

open_questions: IS THIS A DE-RATE OR A DIP? The central question. Today's AI-supply repricing (MU -10.6%, SOXX -6.4%, neoclouds -14/-17%) had a REAL supply-side catalyst (Meta reselling compute + lease rates -31% MoM) — unlike the late-June flushes that were positioning dips bought within days. If it's a de-rate, the supply complex compounds/holds lower (logged as soxx-derate-not-dip + crwv-scarcity-premium-impaired, horizon July 17); if it's a dip, it snaps back like late June. This is the whole book's question — we're supply-heavy.

Does the breakout HOLD through jobs? SPX closed 7,483.23 above the reclaimed 7,450 flip but STALLED (didn't extend to 7,510) and the internal leadership broke. flip-now-support-holds (no close below 7,450) has one session left (July 2 jobs). A close below 7,450 = the breakout was quarter-end air + the AI-doubt re-asserting.

Is the platform-over-supply rotation a REGIME TELL or a one-day artifact? The platforms are the defensive AI trade on capex-doubt days (they'd benefit from cheaper compute). This is the 3rd inverse day for supply-over-demand. Watch whether MSFT/AAPL/GOOGL keep leading (a durable demand-over-supply regime that would challenge the Sept-1 thesis) or the chips snap back.

NEOCLOUD THESIS (T7) STRESS: Meta reselling compute makes the scarcity premium contestable by the customers themselves — the cleanest bear datapoint T7 has taken. CRWV/NBIS both count Meta as a backstop. Does another hyperscaler follow (xAI reportedly already)? A second compute-reseller would be a structural supply shift, not a headline. Flagged for user review (CRWV bull/high, NBIS bull/medium held headless).

RESOLVED: 2026-06-29-warsh-reaction WRONG (Warsh hawkish-leaning but SPX held above 7,450 AND mega-cap-growth ROSE — the hit landed on chips via oversupply, not on platforms via rates). 2026-06-25-aapl-memory-margin WRONG (AAPL reclaimed $293.08 as the memory-buyer bid on a memory-doubt day). YOLO 7,400p NO-TRIGGER (SPX never lost 7,450; avoided a loss; desk 2-2-9).

market_levels.py hung AGAIN (2nd straight session) — the intermittent-hang failure mode is back-to-back. The side-by-side trust period is stalled; snippet/structural stays primary until it runs clean. Re-run next session.
